explainer: The at-rest landing — what the live verified image IS, in prose, with its headline stats.
The moldable inspector (Registry · Spotter · Halo): every object's seven presentation faces; the inspector is itself inspectable.
The inspect→act loop: a cell's reflected state on the left, the messages it understands on the right, each with its cap badge; firing one commits a real turn.
The ocap delegation graph — cells as nodes, capability grants as directed edges, laid out by multi-hop delegation depth.
The cells as a navigable web — the distributed-attestation view of the image.
The object browser — every protocol object by kind.
The STARK/verification axis — the proof status of turns.
Step + explain a turn against the live world — the time-aware debugger.
Deterministic replay / time-travel over the canonical history.
The DOIT/PRINTIT evaluator — a Smalltalk-style workspace over the live image.
The AOL-wonder direct-manipulation surface — click around and absorb, no comprehension required.
The moldable-inspector gadgets (L2–L10): predicate/turn/cap/token construction.
The capability powerbox — granting/brokering authority.
What-links-here — the inbound reference graph for the focused object.
The organ survey — the image's specialized verified-program cells.
The sovereign cipherclerk vault — HD-derived identities, macaroon signing.
The conserving forest editor — build a turn, validate it, commit.
The predicate/caveat composer.
Simulate a turn against a fork before committing.
The agent surface — autonomous loops over the image.
The swarm orchestration surface.
The command shell over the image.
The terminal surface.
The text buffer surface.
The trust panel — identity, guardians, the K-of-N recovery threshold, the KEL timeline.
The document lens — the Pijul-shaped patch-theory document object.
The time/history scrubber.
The sharing/membrane surface.
